name: Crop Over Festival 2026
description: Barbados' biggest cultural festival celebrating the end of the sugar cane harvest. Features calypso, soca music, Kadooment Day parade, and more.

name: Kadooment Day 2026
description: The grand finale of Crop Over — a massive costumed street parade with soca music, DJs, and dancing from the National Stadium to Spring Garden.

name: Oistins Fish Festival 2026
description: Annual Easter weekend festival celebrating Barbados' fishing heritage. Fish-boning competitions, boat races, live music, food stalls, and cultural activities.

name: Reggae Festival Barbados 2026
location: Farley Hill National Park, St. Peter
cost: $75–$200 BBD per day / Weekend pass $350 BBD
description: Three days of reggae, dancehall, and Caribbean music featuring international and local artists in the stunning Farley Hill ruins.

name: Barbados Food & Rum Festival 2026
description: A culinary celebration featuring top Caribbean and international chefs, rum tastings, beach dinners, and cooking demonstrations.

name: Run Barbados Marathon Weekend
description: Annual marathon, half marathon, and 10K run through scenic Bridgetown and along the coast. Open to international runners.

name: Holders Season 2026
description: Performing arts festival featuring opera, Shakespeare, jazz, and comedy in the beautiful gardens of Holders House plantation.

name: Barbados Independence Day Celebrations
description: National holiday celebrating Barbados' independence (1966). Military parade at the Garrison, flag-raising ceremonies, cultural shows, and fireworks.

name: Oistins Friday Night Fish Fry
description: Barbados' most famous weekly liming spot. Fresh grilled fish, music, dancing, and a true local experience every Friday night.

name: Sandy Lane Gold Cup 2026
description: The premier horse racing event in Barbados. A day of exciting races, fashion, and socializing at the historic Garrison Savannah.
